local_qwen.label: right_foot_base

observations.full_context: The full context image shows a person with cyan crop box, indicating the segmentation area.
observations.source_crop: The source crop image provides high-resolution pixels of the subject's body part being segmented.
observations.mask: The proposed binary mask covers the right foot base correctly without including any background or clothing.
observations.overlay: The red overlay over the source shows that the mask accurately covers the right foot base, excluding other anatomical parts and background.
observations.contour: The cyan contour over the source indicates the boundary of the right foot base as proposed by the specialist candidate.
observations.neighbor_overlap: There is no magenta overlap with another protected anatomical label in this image.

local_qwen.evidence: Deterministic topology found 220 components; ontology allows 1.
local_qwen.correction_instruction: Remove disconnected fragments outside the main anatomy.

correction_plan.rationale: Deterministic component count exceeds the ontology maximum.

observations.full_context: The target character-right foot is the rear foot on the viewer's left. The proposal instead emphasizes the large foreground foot on the viewer's right and also contains extensive unrelated body regions.
observations.source_crop: Both feet are visible, but the rear/viewer-left foot is the character's right foot. The foreground/viewer-right foot is the character's left foot.
observations.mask: The binary mask is severely contaminated: it includes hair, torso, hand, and a large foreground foot component rather than an isolated right_foot_base.
observations.overlay: Red overlay covers unrelated upper-body regions and the foreground foot; it does not correctly isolate the character-right rear foot.
observations.contour: The contour includes many disconnected components and follows the wrong foot, with substantial non-target anatomy.
observations.neighbor_overlap: The proposal overlaps the character's left foot and multiple neighboring body parts; the target rear foot is largely missing.

judgment.evidence: The visible character-right foot is the rear foot at the lower-left of the full-person image, while the proposed dominant foot mask is the foreground lower-right foot. The proposal also has major torso, hair, and hand contamination.

correction.rationale: Use positive points across the visible rear/viewer-left foot and ankle transition, and negative points on the foreground foot and unrelated body components. SAM refinement is safer than a polygon because the rear foot is partially occluded/overlapped near the other leg and lies close to the floor/background.

observations.full_context: No green specialist contour visible on full-context image; overall pose shows a distorted/limb-scrambled figure making per-part attribution unreliable.
observations.source_crop: Source crop shows same distorted figure; foot regions are small and ambiguous at full-body scale, no clear right_foot_base isolation visible.
observations.mask: Binary mask shows large disconnected white blobs covering head/shoulder area and a hand-shaped region, plus a thin sliver at bottom near feet - mask geometry does not correspond to a single foot region and instead spans multiple unrelated body areas.
observations.overlay: Red overlay covers torso, shoulder, arm, and hand areas plus a thin strip near feet - this is clearly not isolated to right_foot_base, showing massive over-inclusion of neighboring parts (shoulder, arm, torso).
observations.contour: Contour lines trace torso/arm/hand boundaries, not a foot outline; this confirms the mask is mislabeled/misassigned to right_foot_base.
observations.neighbor_overlap: Neighbor overlap image identical to overlay, confirming overlap with shoulder, upper arm, forearm, hand_base, and torso regions - severe includes_neighbor_part contamination.

judgment.evidence: The proposed mask for right_foot_base is almost entirely composed of unrelated body regions (shoulder, arm, hand, torso) rather than the visible right foot. Auto-QA deterministic overrides confirm component_count_exceeds_ontology, area_sanity failure (12.81% vs expected 0.2-4%), components_limit violation (220 components), side_coherence flip, and chain_integrity break. This is a catastrophic segmentation failure, not a minor boundary issue.

correction.rationale: The mask bears no resemblance to a foot region and instead spans shoulder/arm/hand/torso areas with 220 disconnected components, exceeding ontology limits and area sanity bounds by >6x. This requires full human re-annotation rather than automated point/polygon correction; cloud proposal is unusable for guiding a fix.
